en.changeSummary: {ch1}A severe thunderstorm is now ongoing, bringing heavy rain, hail, and strong gusty winds — conditions are significantly more hazardous than the rain-only forecast previously communicated. Expect dangerous storm activity through at least mid-afternoon local time ({q:time:2026-04-21T18:00:00Z}), with winds already gusting to {q:gust:25} and conditions likely to remain unsettled.
en.closing: In summary: the remainder of today looks stormy and dangerous through {q:time:2026-04-21T18:00:00Z}, with a severe thunderstorm producing heavy rain, hail, and strong gusts. Conditions are expected to ease later this evening, transitioning back to a wet but non-severe outlook. Stay indoors and away from windows until the storm clears.

input.reasoning_trace:
Step 1 — TAF vs GFS: The TAF was issued at 2026-04-21T11:38Z, after the GFS run at 06Z, so the TAF prevails within its validity window. The TAF base group shows overcast/scattered with showers; the 15Z amendment brings light rain showers; the 30% probability group from 19–23Z indicates possible thunderstorm and rain. The TAF alone does not indicate severe storms.

Step 2 — Current observation override: The 13:53Z observation (within the 12–18Z block) reports a severe thunderstorm with heavy rain, hail, cumulonimbus at 2500 ft, visibility sharply reduced, winds 25 mph gusting 47 mph (approx. 22 kt sustained, 41 kt gusts). This is active, observed weather in the current block window (12–18Z). The observation overrides the provisional for the 12–18Z block: precipPhenomenon upgraded to thunderstorm, severeFlag set to true (hail + damaging gusts are observed), wind range updated to reflect observed conditions (~18–25 kt to capture the gust range credibly within the snapshot schema). The 18–00Z block is left at likely/rain/severeFlag false per model/TAF guidance (no further severe signal beyond the 30% probabilistic TS group, which is not deterministic).

Step 3 — Comparison to prior_snapshot: The prior 12–18Z block was precipExpectation likely, precipPhenomenon rain, severeFlag false. The reconciled block is now precipPhenomenon thunderstorm, severeFlag true — a clear upgrade to safety-critical. This is a safety-critical change (hail, damaging gusts, severe thunderstorm actively occurring) and is unambiguously news worth sending despite changed_since_last_sent_report being "observation only." The directional asymmetry rule confirms: weather (severe storm) is ARRIVING that the prior forecast did not promise. The anti-reversal rule does not apply here because we are ADDING a hazard, not reversing/removing one. Submit_reconciled_report is warranted.
